name: Notion
known_strengths: Strong SME usability and quick onboarding
Flexible workspace for docs, tasks, lightweight databases
Recognizable vendor with public security and privacy information
known_risks: Workspace governance can become complex as teams grow
Structured data export and migration should be checked before storing critical client records

name: Airtable
known_strengths: Excellent fit for structured workflows and operational databases
Flexible automations and integrations
Good choice when the business process is table-centric
known_risks: Costs can rise with scale, seats, records, and advanced features
Data models and automations may create meaningful workflow lock-in

name: Coda
known_strengths: Flexible document-app model
Good for teams that want docs, tables, and lightweight workflows together
Pricing can be appealing for small teams depending on maker/editor mix
known_risks: Less obvious default choice for non-technical client-project operations
Workflow portability should be checked if documents become app-like

name: Asana
known_strengths: Purpose-built project management workflow
Good task tracking, accountability, and collaboration features
known_risks: May be too project-management-specific if the team also needs a knowledge base
Advanced admin/security features can be plan-dependent

name: ClickUp
known_strengths: Broad project management feature set
Good value proposition for teams wanting many features in one tool
known_risks: Feature breadth can increase configuration and adoption complexity
Migration risk increases if a team deeply customizes workflows

name: Monday.com
known_strengths: Strong visual workflow management
Good template ecosystem for SMB operations
known_risks: Pricing and plan limits should be checked against expected team growth
Highly customized boards can create process lock-in

name: Slack
known_strengths: Very strong collaboration and integration ecosystem
Mature operational footprint
known_risks: Not a system of record for structured client project data
Knowledge can become fragmented across channels

name: HubSpot
known_strengths: Strong CRM and go-to-market platform for SMEs
Good documentation and operational maturity
known_risks: Costs can increase significantly as hubs, contacts, and seats grow
Deep CRM adoption can create meaningful migration cost

name: Google Workspace
known_strengths: Mature productivity suite with broad SME adoption
Strong admin ecosystem and documentation
known_risks: Vendor concentration risk if the business depends heavily on the ecosystem
Admin/security setup still requires deliberate configuration

name: Microsoft 365
known_strengths: Mature enterprise-grade productivity and identity ecosystem
Strong security/admin tooling when configured well
known_risks: Can be complex for small teams without admin expertise
Plan selection and licensing can be confusing

name: Linear
known_strengths: Fast issue tracking for product and engineering teams
Good developer-focused UX
known_risks: Narrower fit outside product/engineering workflows
Should not be treated as a general client-data system of record
